methods.description: Process for collecting and recording atmospheric pollen concentrations at two locations in the Netherlands to study the impact of seasonal climate variables on annual pollen production.

steps.description: Atmospheric pollen concentrations have been recorded daily since AD 1969 at LUMC and since AD 1975 at Elkerliek Hospital.
steps.searchable_summary: record daily atmospheric pollen concentrations at LUMC and Elkerliek Hospital

steps.description: Daily pollen counts were summed to determine total annual pollen influx.
steps.searchable_summary: sum daily pollen counts to calculate total annual pollen influx

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected pollen data to determine the influence of climate variables on pollen production.

steps.description: Pollen counts were square-root transformed as Q-Q plots and non-parametric Kolmogorov-Smirnov testing showed a nonnormal distribution for most pollen types.
steps.searchable_summary: apply square-root transformation to pollen counts

steps.description: A principal component analysis (PCA) of the LUMC square-root transformed pollen counts was performed using CANOCO software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on transformed pollen counts using CANOCO

steps.description: A two-tailed Spearman rank correlation was performed to assess which pollen types correlate significantly with climate.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Spearman rank correlation between pollen types and climate variables

steps.description: Linear regression analysis with time as a predictor, and multiple regression analysis with climatological variables as predictors were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear and multiple regression analysis on pollen data

steps.description: Prediction skill of the resulting model was tested by random division of the dataset in a calibration and an independent test set.
steps.searchable_summary: validate regression model using calibration and test datasets

methods.description: Development and validation of a predictive regression model to assess the effect of meteorological variables on pollen influx.

steps.description: Multiple regression analysis with backward selection was used to assess the effect of meteorological variables on pollen influx.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple regression analysis with backward selection on pollen influx data

steps.description: Calibration and prediction skill of the resulting optimal model were tested.
steps.searchable_summary: test calibration and prediction skill of regression model

steps.description: Multitaper spectral analysis was used to evaluate cyclicity patterns and autocorrelations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multitaper spectral analysis for cyclicity and autocorrelation evaluation
